name: Route load latency across useful ShelLeport GET routes and new-session creation

description: Baseline route benchmark harness established; benchmark passes, but existing formatting issues block keeping until formatting is fixed or benchmark files are formatted.

name: Claude turn latency via persistent per-session process reuse

description: Baseline for Claude turn latency established through real HTTP+SSE sessions with the local Claude CLI. Repeated turns are still spawn-bound at ~3.7s each, while a manual direct persistent CLI stays warm at ~8ms; checks script failed because it invoked oxfmt directly instead of via bunx.

Tried a persistent per-session Claude subprocess using stream-json stdin/stdout plus targeted fake-CLI reuse coverage. Correctness passed, but real CLI conversation latency regressed badly under the current unauthenticated workload despite direct persistent CLI still beating fresh spawn. Discarding this version and keeping the finding that process reuse is not automatically a win with the current integration.

Tested disabling Claude slash commands on every ShelLeport turn to reduce CLI startup work. Real conversation latency regressed sharply and the direct CLI microcheck showed essentially no benefit, so slash-command loading is not the main bottleneck here.

name: Claude turn latency with opt-in bare-mode launch

description: Added an opt-in Claude bare-mode launch path via SHELLEPORT_CLAUDE_BARE=1 and benchmarked ShelLeport end-to-end with that mode enabled. This is a large real improvement for the current startup-bound workload: per-turn latency dropped from multi-second spawn-bound behavior to ~0.71s while remaining explicitly non-default so normal managed-session semantics are unchanged unless the operator opts in.
